cases.title: App fetches hosted catalog and verifies its signature on launch
preconditions: A signed catalog envelope {payload, signature} is published to the hosted GitHub Pages URL
A signed key-ring is published and resolves to the operational key that signed the catalog
The app ships with the embedded bootstrap root key anchoring the key-ring

steps.instruction: Launch the app with network access to the hosted marketplace URL

observations.expected: The app requests the catalog from the hosted URL, not from an embedded file

steps.instruction: Let the app fetch the signed key-ring and verify it against the embedded bootstrap root key

observations.expected: The key-ring signature validates against the embedded root key and the operational key is resolved

steps.instruction: Let the app verify the fetched catalog's ed25519 signature against the resolved operational key

observations.expected: The catalog signature verifies successfully
The catalog payload is accepted and parsed

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace Browse screen

observations.expected: Verified catalog entries are listed

cases.title: Browse screen filters verified catalog entries by Integrations and Components
preconditions: The app has fetched and signature-verified the hosted catalog
The Browse screen is open with a mix of Integration and Component entries

steps.instruction: Type a query into the catalog search box

observations.expected: The listing filters to entries matching the query

steps.instruction: Select the Integrations filter

observations.expected: Only Integration-type entries are shown

steps.instruction: Select the Components filter

observations.expected: Only Component-type entries are shown

cases.title: Maintainer CI builds, signs, digests, and publishes a plugin plus regenerated signed catalog
preconditions: The marketplace repo CI pipeline is configured with the ed25519 signing key available only as a CI secret
A plugin source change is ready to publish

steps.instruction: Trigger the marketplace CI pipeline for the plugin change
Let CI build the plugin artifact

observations.expected: A per-plugin artifact is produced

steps.instruction: Let CI compute the artifact digest and sign the artifact

observations.expected: A digest is computed for the artifact
The artifact is signed with the operational signing key

steps.instruction: Let CI regenerate and sign the catalog and publish artifact plus catalog

observations.expected: The artifact is published as a per-plugin GitHub Release asset
The regenerated catalog is re-signed and published to the hosted GitHub Pages URL
The catalog entry references the published artifact digest

cases.title: Maintainer revokes a catalog entry and clients block it at next refresh
preconditions: The catalog contains an active entry that is installable
The maintainer has access to publish a revised signed catalog without an app release

steps.instruction: Set revoked:true on the target entry, re-sign the catalog, and republish it

observations.expected: The republished catalog carries the revoked entry with a valid signature

steps.instruction: On a client, refresh the catalog by reopening the marketplace

observations.expected: The newly fetched catalog signature verifies

steps.instruction: Locate the revoked entry in the Browse listing

observations.expected: The revoked entry is either removed from listings or shown with its Install button disabled
Install and update are blocked for the revoked entry

cases.title: Maintainer rotates the signing key without an app release and clients accept the new key
preconditions: A key-ring signed by the root key currently anchors one operational key
A new operational key pair has been generated for rotation

steps.instruction: Add the new operational public key to the key-ring and re-sign the ring with the root key

observations.expected: The republished key-ring validates against the embedded root key and includes the new operational key

steps.instruction: Re-sign the catalog with the new operational key and republish it

observations.expected: The catalog is published signed by the new operational key, with no app release performed

steps.instruction: On a client, refresh by reopening the marketplace

observations.expected: The client resolves the new operational key from the verified key-ring
The catalog signed by the new key verifies and entries list normally

cases.title: Catalog with a tampered or invalid signature is rejected
preconditions: A signed key-ring and operational key are resolvable by the app
A catalog whose payload has been modified after signing (or whose signature bytes are corrupted) is served from the hosted URL

steps.instruction: Launch the app and let it fetch the tampered catalog

observations.expected: Ed25519 signature verification against the operational key fails

steps.instruction: Observe the app's handling of the failed verification

observations.expected: The tampered catalog payload is rejected and not used to populate listings
No entries from the tampered catalog are offered for install

cases.title: A revoked entry cannot be installed even via direct action
preconditions: The verified catalog marks the target entry revoked:true
The Browse screen shows the revoked entry with its Install button disabled

steps.instruction: Attempt to install the revoked entry (via the disabled Install control or a direct install request)

observations.expected: Install is blocked for the revoked entry

steps.instruction: Attempt to update an already-installed copy of the revoked plugin

observations.expected: Update is blocked for the revoked entry

cases.title: Catalog signed by a key not present in the key-ring is rejected
preconditions: The app has a valid key-ring anchored by the embedded root key
A catalog is served signed by an operational key that is NOT present in the key-ring

steps.instruction: Launch the app and let it fetch the catalog signed by the unknown key

observations.expected: No key in the verified key-ring matches the catalog signer

steps.instruction: Observe verification handling

observations.expected: Catalog verification fails because the signing key is unknown/untrusted
The catalog is rejected and entries are not listed or installed

cases.title: Tampered key-ring not signed by the embedded root key is rejected
preconditions: The app ships with the embedded bootstrap root key
A key-ring whose contents were altered after signing (or signed by a non-root key) is served from the hosted URL

steps.instruction: Launch the app and let it fetch the tampered key-ring

observations.expected: Key-ring signature verification against the embedded root key fails

steps.instruction: Observe how the app proceeds with catalog resolution

observations.expected: The tampered key-ring is rejected and no operational key is trusted from it
The catalog is not accepted and no entries are offered for install

cases.title: Catalog exceeding the size budget is handled without crashing
preconditions: The hosted URL serves a signed catalog whose size exceeds the configured size budget

steps.instruction: Launch the app and let it begin fetching the oversized catalog

observations.expected: The fetch is bounded by the size budget rather than consuming unbounded memory

steps.instruction: Observe the app's response when the size budget is exceeded

observations.expected: The oversized catalog is rejected with a clear failure rather than a crash or hang
The app remains responsive and no partial/unverified entries are installed

cases.title: Signing key never appears in CI logs or published artifacts, and revocation propagates without an app release
preconditions: The ed25519 signing key is configured only as a CI secret / held out-of-band
A publish pipeline run and a revocation publish have completed

steps.instruction: Run a secret-scan across the published artifacts (Release assets, catalog, key-ring)

observations.expected: No ed25519 private key material is present in any published artifact

steps.instruction: Run a secret-scan across the CI pipeline logs for the publish run

observations.expected: No private signing key material is emitted or echoed in CI logs

steps.instruction: Publish a revocation (revoked:true + re-sign + republish) and refresh a client at next catalog fetch

observations.expected: The revoked plugin is blocked on the client at the next catalog refresh with no app release shipped

cases.title: Published artifact digest mismatch is detected and install blocked
preconditions: The verified catalog lists an entry with a recorded artifact digest
The corresponding GitHub Release asset has been replaced with content whose digest differs from the catalog

steps.instruction: Initiate install of the entry and let the app download the Release asset

observations.expected: The downloaded artifact's computed digest does not match the catalog-recorded digest

steps.instruction: Observe the install handling on digest mismatch

observations.expected: Install is aborted and the mismatched artifact is not used

cases.title: Add a new catalog entry without an app release and clients see it at next refresh
preconditions: A new plugin artifact has been published as a Release asset
The maintainer can publish a revised signed catalog without an app release

steps.instruction: Add the new entry to the catalog, re-sign, and republish to the hosted URL

observations.expected: The republished catalog includes the new entry and verifies

steps.instruction: On a client, refresh by reopening the marketplace

observations.expected: The new entry appears in the Browse listing without an app release
The new entry is installable

cases.title: End-to-end: maintainer publishes then revokes a plugin and a client reflects both at next refresh
preconditions: The marketplace repo CI is configured with the signing key as a CI secret
A client app instance with the embedded bootstrap root key is available
GitHub Pages hosting and the key-ring are reachable

steps.instruction: As maintainer, trigger CI to build, sign, and digest a new plugin artifact

observations.expected: The artifact is published as a GitHub Release asset with a computed digest

steps.instruction: Let CI regenerate, re-sign, and publish the catalog with the new entry

observations.expected: The signed catalog at the hosted URL now includes the new entry

steps.instruction: As a client user, launch the app and open the marketplace Browse screen

observations.expected: The app fetches the catalog and verifies its signature via the key-ring anchored by the embedded root key

steps.instruction: Find the newly published entry and install it

observations.expected: The entry is listed and installs successfully after digest verification

steps.instruction: As maintainer, set revoked:true on the entry, re-sign the catalog, and republish (no app release)

observations.expected: The republished signed catalog marks the entry revoked

steps.instruction: As the client user, refresh by reopening the marketplace

observations.expected: The refreshed catalog verifies and the entry is shown revoked (or removed) with Install disabled
Install and update of the revoked entry are blocked at this next refresh with no app release shipped

cases.title: Installed plugin runs from dist/index.js with no build step
preconditions: Marketplace catalog is signature-verified and available
A pre-built plugin artifact is published as a Release asset tarball

steps.instruction: Install the plugin from the marketplace listing

observations.expected: Install completes without invoking any compiler, bundler, or build script
~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/dist/index.js exists on disk after install

steps.instruction: Invoke the installed plugin entry point

observations.expected: dist/index.js loads and runs directly with no transpilation or build step on the user's machine

cases.title: Full download to install happy path produces a runnable plugin
preconditions: App holds the catalog signing public key (ed25519)
A valid, untampered plugin artifact and catalog are published

steps.instruction: Trigger install on the Install & verify screen

observations.expected: Step 1 'Download built artifact' completes and the Release asset tarball is fetched into staging

steps.instruction: Let the catalog signature verification step run

observations.expected: Step 2 'Verify catalog signature ed25519' passes against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Let the artifact digest verification step run

observations.expected: Step 3 'Verify artifact digest sha256' passes (computed digest over the built package matches the catalog value)

steps.instruction: Let the unpack and install step complete

observations.expected: Step 4 'Unpack & install' atomically renames staging into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>
All four progress steps show success and the plugin appears as installed and runnable

cases.title: End-to-end install journey from marketplace listing to running plugin
preconditions: App holds the catalog signing public key
Marketplace catalog lists an untampered, signature-verified plugin with a built-artifact digest

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace and select the plugin listing

observations.expected: Listing is shown because the catalog signature already verified against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Click Install to open the Install & verify screen

observations.expected: The 4-step progress (Download, Verify catalog signature, Verify artifact digest, Unpack & install) is displayed

steps.instruction: Wait for 'Download built artifact' to finish

observations.expected: The per-plugin Release asset tarball is downloaded into staging with no git clone

steps.instruction: Wait for 'Verify catalog signature ed25519' to finish

observations.expected: Catalog signature passes ed25519 verification against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Wait for 'Verify artifact digest sha256' to finish

observations.expected: Computed sha256 over the built artifact matches the catalog digest

steps.instruction: Wait for 'Unpack & install' to finish

observations.expected: Staging is atomically renamed into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id> and dist/index.js is present

steps.instruction: Invoke the installed plugin

observations.expected: dist/index.js runs directly with no build step on the user's machine
Plugin is listed as installed and is fully usable

cases.title: Digest mismatch on built artifact is rejected fail-closed
preconditions: Catalog signature verifies successfully
Downloaded tarball's built artifact has been modified so its sha256 no longer matches the catalog digest

steps.instruction: Run install through to the 'Verify artifact digest sha256' step

observations.expected: Step 3 fails: computed digest over the built package does not match the catalog digest

steps.instruction: Observe the install outcome after the digest failure

observations.expected: Install aborts with 'digest mismatch, nothing written, nothing executed'
No directory is created under ~/.roubo/plugins/<id> and no plugin code is executed

cases.title: Bad catalog signature is rejected before any download or install
preconditions: Catalog content or its ed25519 signature has been altered so it no longer verifies against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Attempt to load the marketplace catalog

observations.expected: Catalog signature verification fails against the app-held ed25519 key

steps.instruction: Attempt to install a plugin from the unverified catalog

observations.expected: No listing is served and install is refused (fail-closed)
No artifact is downloaded, written, or executed

cases.title: Zip-slip / path-escaping tarball entry is rejected during unpack
preconditions: Catalog signature verifies and digest matches a tarball that contains an entry whose path escapes the staging root (e.g. ../../etc/evil or an absolute path)

steps.instruction: Unpack the tarball into staging with the path-containment guard active

observations.expected: The path-escaping entry is detected by the zip-slip guard and rejected

steps.instruction: Observe the filesystem outside the staging root

observations.expected: No file is written outside the staging/install root
Install aborts and nothing is renamed into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>

cases.title: Oversized tarball exceeding size/entry-count limits is rejected
preconditions: A tarball whose total unpacked size or entry count exceeds the configured staging limits is presented for install

steps.instruction: Begin unpacking the tarball into staging

observations.expected: Unpack aborts once the size limit or entry-count limit is exceeded

steps.instruction: Observe the install outcome and staging directory

observations.expected: Install fails closed with no plugin written to ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>
Partial staging contents are cleaned up and nothing is executed

cases.title: Tampered artifact is rejected with zero unverified execution (no-network tamper test)
preconditions: Offline / no-network tamper harness (mirrors marketplace-integrity.test.ts)
Catalog signature is valid but the built artifact bytes have been tampered after digest publication

steps.instruction: Run the install verification pipeline against the tampered artifact with no network access

observations.expected: sha256 digest over the built package fails to match the catalog digest

steps.instruction: Assert post-conditions of the rejection

observations.expected: The tampered artifact is rejected (install aborts)
0 unverified artifacts are executed and nothing is written to ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>

cases.title: Tampered catalog is rejected with zero unverified execution (no-network tamper test)
preconditions: Offline / no-network tamper harness (mirrors marketplace-integrity.test.ts)
Catalog has been tampered so its ed25519 signature no longer verifies against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Run catalog signature verification against the tampered catalog with no network access

observations.expected: ed25519 signature verification fails against the app-held key

steps.instruction: Assert post-conditions of the rejection

observations.expected: The tampered catalog is rejected: no listing served and no install permitted
0 unverified artifacts are executed and nothing is downloaded or written

cases.title: Tamper rejection demo aborts the install at the digest step
preconditions: Install & verify screen is open with the 'Show tamper rejection' action available

steps.instruction: Trigger the 'Show tamper rejection' action

observations.expected: Steps 1 and 2 (download, catalog signature) pass and step 3 'Verify artifact digest sha256' fails

steps.instruction: Read the abort message and final state

observations.expected: Install aborts with the message 'digest mismatch, nothing written, nothing executed'
Step 4 'Unpack & install' never runs and no plugin is installed

cases.title: Truncated download is rejected and nothing is installed
preconditions: The Release asset download is interrupted so the tarball is incomplete/truncated

steps.instruction: Attempt to download the built-artifact tarball and have the connection cut mid-transfer

observations.expected: Download is detected as incomplete (truncated tarball / failed read)

steps.instruction: Continue the verification pipeline with the truncated tarball

observations.expected: Verification fails (unreadable archive or digest mismatch) and install aborts fail-closed
No plugin is written to ~/.roubo/plugins/<id> and nothing is executed

cases.title: Partial unpack failure rolls back staging leaving install root untouched
preconditions: Unpack into staging fails partway (e.g. disk error or a late-detected limit/path violation after some entries are written)

steps.instruction: Start unpacking the tarball into staging and induce a mid-unpack failure

observations.expected: The atomic rename into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id> never occurs because unpack did not complete

steps.instruction: Inspect staging and the install root after the failure

observations.expected: Partial staging contents are rolled back / cleaned up
~/.roubo/plugins/<id> is either absent or unchanged from its prior state; no partial install is exposed or executed

cases.title: Reinstall over an existing plugin is atomic and never exposes a half-written dist
preconditions: A verified version of the plugin is already installed at ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>
A new verified artifact is being installed over it

steps.instruction: Install the new verified artifact while the previous version is present

observations.expected: New artifact is verified (signature + digest) before any change to the install root

steps.instruction: Observe the install root during and after the atomic rename

observations.expected: The swap is atomic: at no point is a partially-written dist/index.js observable at ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>
After install, dist/index.js is the new verified build and runs with no build step

cases.title: Fresh launch yields exactly the three seeded plugins, usable with no network
preconditions: Clean machine: no ~/.roubo/plugins directory and no seed idempotency marker
Installer shipped resources/seed/ with the three built+signed tarballs and a signed seed-catalog snapshot
Network is fully disabled (airplane mode / no DNS, no egress)

steps.instruction: Launch the app for the first time on the clean machine

observations.expected: App reaches a usable/ready state without any network request on the critical path
plugin-manager.seedFromBundled() runs and installs into ~/.roubo/plugins

steps.instruction: Open the installed-plugins view

observations.expected: Exactly three plugins are present: github-com, process, and database
Each of the three is shown as 'Seeded' and 'Verified'
No other plugin (e.g. ghe, jira-self-hosted) is installed

steps.instruction: Confirm a seeded plugin (e.g. process) is usable by invoking it offline

observations.expected: The seeded plugin is operational without any network call

cases.title: First-run seed installs offline and surfaces the confirmation toast
preconditions: Clean machine with no seed idempotency marker
Network disabled

steps.instruction: Launch the app on the clean machine with no network

observations.expected: A toast appears reading 'First-run seed installed (offline, verified): github-com, process, database'
A seed idempotency marker is written after the install completes

cases.title: Seeded plugins verify against the bundled signed seed catalog
preconditions: Clean machine with no seed idempotency marker
Network disabled so verification cannot reach the hosted catalog

steps.instruction: Launch the app to trigger seedFromBundled()
Inspect the verification result recorded for each seeded plugin

observations.expected: github-com, process, and database each verify against the signed seed-catalog snapshot (not the hosted catalog)
All three are marked 'Verified' and signature/digest checks pass offline

cases.title: ghe installs on demand from the marketplace after seeding
preconditions: App has completed first-run seed (github-com, process, database installed)
Network is available and the hosted catalog is reachable
ghe is shown as 'Not installed (marketplace-only)'

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace and select ghe

observations.expected: ghe is listed as marketplace-only and installable on demand

steps.instruction: Trigger install of ghe

observations.expected: ghe is fetched from the hosted catalog, verified, and placed into ~/.roubo/plugins
ghe transitions from 'Not installed (marketplace-only)' to installed/verified

cases.title: jira-self-hosted installs on demand from the marketplace
preconditions: First-run seed already completed
Network available and hosted catalog reachable
jira-self-hosted shown as 'Not installed (marketplace-only)'

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace and trigger install of jira-self-hosted

observations.expected: jira-self-hosted is fetched, verified, and placed into ~/.roubo/plugins
jira-self-hosted becomes installed/verified and usable

cases.title: Seed runs only once: second launch is idempotent and does not re-seed
preconditions: App has already completed first-run seed and written the idempotency marker
All three seeded plugins remain installed in ~/.roubo/plugins

steps.instruction: Relaunch the app

observations.expected: seedFromBundled() detects the idempotency marker and does not re-install the seeded plugins
No 'First-run seed installed' toast is shown on the second launch

steps.instruction: Inspect the installed-plugins view after relaunch

observations.expected: Still exactly github-com, process, database (no duplicates, no overwrite of marketplace-managed updates)

cases.title: Marketplace-managed update to a seeded plugin is not clobbered by re-seed
preconditions: First-run seed completed and idempotency marker written
A seeded plugin (e.g. github-com) has since been updated to a newer version via the marketplace

steps.instruction: Relaunch the app after the marketplace update

observations.expected: seedFromBundled() does not run again and does not downgrade the marketplace-updated github-com back to the bundled seed version
The marketplace-managed version of github-com is preserved

cases.title: Corrupt seed artifact is rejected fail-closed (not installed)
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker
One bundled seed tarball (e.g. database) is tampered so its signature/digest no longer matches the signed seed catalog

steps.instruction: Launch the app to trigger seedFromBundled() with the corrupted artifact present

observations.expected: The tampered artifact fails verification against the signed seed catalog
The corrupt plugin is NOT installed into ~/.roubo/plugins (fail-closed)
An error/warning is surfaced indicating the seed artifact was rejected

steps.instruction: Inspect the installed-plugins view

observations.expected: The rejected plugin (database) is absent; no partial/unverified copy is placed

cases.title: ghe and jira-self-hosted are NOT present in the seed on a clean install
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker
Network disabled (so nothing can be fetched from the hosted catalog)

steps.instruction: Launch the app offline to complete the first-run seed
Inspect the installed-plugins view and the seeded artifact set

observations.expected: ghe is shown as 'Not installed (marketplace-only)' and has no bundled seed artifact
jira-self-hosted is shown as 'Not installed (marketplace-only)' and has no bundled seed artifact
Only github-com, process, database were installed by the seed

cases.title: Missing or empty bundled seed directory does not block app readiness
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker
resources/seed/ is missing or contains no tarballs (degraded installer scenario)

steps.instruction: Launch the app

observations.expected: seedFromBundled() handles the absent/empty seed gracefully without crashing the app
App still reaches a usable state; no partial install is left behind and no idempotency marker falsely claims a successful seed

cases.title: Partial seed interrupted then resumed completes to exactly three verified plugins
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker
First launch is interrupted (process killed) after one or two of the three plugins are installed but before the idempotency marker is written

steps.instruction: Start the app, then kill it mid-seed after only github-com (and possibly process) have been placed

observations.expected: No idempotency marker is present because the seed did not complete

steps.instruction: Relaunch the app

observations.expected: seedFromBundled() resumes and installs the remaining seeded plugin(s) (verifying each)
Any partially-written/unverified artifact from the interrupted run is not left in a usable state and is re-verified or replaced

steps.instruction: Inspect the installed-plugins view and idempotency marker after the resumed run

observations.expected: Exactly github-com, process, database are installed and all marked 'Verified'
The idempotency marker is now written so subsequent launches do not re-seed

cases.title: Concurrent launches do not double-seed or race the idempotency marker
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker
Two app instances (or two windows) start nearly simultaneously on first run

steps.instruction: Launch two instances at the same time so both observe no idempotency marker

observations.expected: Seeding is serialized/locked so the three plugins are installed once, not duplicated or corrupted by a race

steps.instruction: Inspect ~/.roubo/plugins and the idempotency marker after both instances settle

observations.expected: Exactly github-com, process, database present, each 'Verified'; exactly one idempotency marker written

cases.title: First-run readiness < 5s offline, catalog payload < 256 KB, single install p95 < 10s @ 10 Mbps
preconditions: Clean machine with no idempotency marker for the offline readiness measurement
Network shaped to 10 Mbps for the single-install benchmark
Bundled seed-catalog snapshot and hosted catalog payload available for size measurement

steps.instruction: With network fully disabled, time from app launch to first-run readiness (seed installed and usable)

observations.expected: Readiness is reached in < 5s
No network call occurs on the critical path (verified by network trace showing zero egress during readiness)

steps.instruction: Measure the size of the catalog fetch payload (hosted catalog snapshot used for marketplace)

observations.expected: Catalog fetch payload is < 256 KB

steps.instruction: Benchmark a single on-demand plugin install (fetch + verify + place) at 10 Mbps across repeated runs

observations.expected: Single-plugin install (fetch+verify+place) completes at p95 < 10s @ 10 Mbps

cases.title: E2E first-run journey: fresh launch to three seeded plugins, usable offline
preconditions: Brand-new clean machine: no ~/.roubo/plugins, no idempotency marker
Network is fully disabled for the entire flow (airplane mode)

steps.instruction: Install the app from the installer that ships resources/seed/ (three signed tarballs + signed seed-catalog snapshot)
Launch the app for the first time with no network

observations.expected: App reaches a usable state with no network call on the critical path
Toast appears: 'First-run seed installed (offline, verified): github-com, process, database'

steps.instruction: Open the Fresh install / installed-plugins screen

observations.expected: github-com, process, database each show 'Seeded' and 'Verified'
ghe and jira-self-hosted each show 'Not installed (marketplace-only)'

steps.instruction: Exercise a seeded plugin (e.g. create a bench using the process plugin) while still offline

observations.expected: The seeded plugin operates correctly with zero network access

steps.instruction: Close and relaunch the app (still offline)

observations.expected: No re-seed occurs and no first-run toast reappears (idempotent)
The same three plugins remain Seeded + Verified and usable

cases.title: App loads seeded and previously-installed plugins with the marketplace unreachable
preconditions: App has completed first run online at least once
At least one seeded plugin and one previously-installed (non-seeded) plugin exist
A last-verified signed catalog envelope is cached under ~/.roubo/marketplace/
Network to the hosted marketplace is unreachable

steps.instruction: Disconnect the network so the hosted catalog endpoint is unreachable
Launch the app and open the Plugins view

observations.expected: The app starts without crashing or hanging
The plugin list is non-empty (never zero plugins)

steps.instruction: Inspect the status of a seeded plugin and a previously-installed plugin

observations.expected: The seeded plugin shows a Running/available state
The previously-installed plugin shows a Running/available state

cases.title: Catalog degrades to the last-known cached envelope and shows the offline banner
preconditions: App has completed first run online
A last-verified signed catalog envelope was cached 2h ago under ~/.roubo/marketplace/
Network to the hosted marketplace is unreachable

steps.instruction: Open the Plugins view while the marketplace fetch fails on a network error

observations.expected: The catalog renders from the last-known cached envelope, not the seed catalog
Plugins from the cached catalog are listed

steps.instruction: Read the warning banner at the top of the Plugins view

observations.expected: Banner states the marketplace is unreachable and the last verified catalog is being shown
Banner indicates how stale the cache is (e.g. fetched 2h ago)
Banner notes seeded and installed plugins are available and new installs are paused until back online

cases.title: On a network fetch failure the catalog-client returns the cached envelope
preconditions: A valid signed catalog envelope is cached under ~/.roubo/marketplace/
The marketplace HTTP endpoint returns a connection/timeout error

steps.instruction: Invoke the catalog-client fetch with the marketplace endpoint unreachable

observations.expected: The fetch failure is caught and not surfaced as an unhandled exception
The cached catalog envelope is returned to the caller
The source of the returned catalog is reported as the on-disk cache, not the live network

cases.title: Attempting to install a new plugin offline fails with a clear message and no crash
preconditions: Network to the marketplace is unreachable
The Plugins view is showing the last-known cached catalog
Jira is a non-seeded plugin that is not yet installed

steps.instruction: Click Install on the Jira plugin

observations.expected: The install attempt fails fast and does not crash the app
A clear error is shown, e.g. "Can't install while the marketplace is unreachable"
No partial or corrupt install artifacts are left behind for Jira

steps.instruction: Re-check the seeded and previously-installed plugins after the failed install

observations.expected: Seeded and installed plugins remain Running/available and unaffected

cases.title: A cached catalog with a bad signature is not trusted and verification stays fail-closed
preconditions: Network to the marketplace is unreachable
The on-disk cached catalog envelope under ~/.roubo/marketplace/ has an invalid/tampered signature

steps.instruction: Open the Plugins view so the catalog-client loads the cached envelope and verifies its signature

observations.expected: Signature verification of the cached envelope fails
The tampered cached catalog is rejected and not served as the catalog
The client falls through to the trusted seed catalog rather than trusting unsigned/tampered data

steps.instruction: Verify the plugin list after the bad-signature cache is rejected

observations.expected: The plugin list is non-empty (seed catalog is shown, never zero plugins)
Verification remains fail-closed: nothing from the rejected cache is treated as verified

cases.title: Offline catalog degradation never falls to zero plugins
preconditions: App has completed first run online
Network to the marketplace is unreachable

steps.instruction: Force every catalog source to its worst case in turn: live fetch fails, then evaluate with cache present and with cache absent

observations.expected: With a valid cache present, the cached catalog is served (non-empty)
With no usable cache, the seed catalog is served (non-empty)

steps.instruction: Assert the rendered plugin count across both source paths

observations.expected: The plugin count is always >= the seed catalog size and is never zero

cases.title: Corrupt or empty on-disk cache falls through to the seed catalog
preconditions: Network to the marketplace is unreachable
The cached catalog file under ~/.roubo/marketplace/ is empty or contains truncated/non-parseable JSON

steps.instruction: Open the Plugins view so the catalog-client attempts to read the corrupt/empty cache

observations.expected: Reading/parsing the corrupt cache fails without crashing the app
The client falls through to the seed catalog
The plugin list is non-empty (seed plugins are shown)

cases.title: Catalog fetch returning a bad-signature payload falls back to the cached catalog
preconditions: A valid signed catalog envelope is cached under ~/.roubo/marketplace/
The marketplace is reachable but returns a payload whose signature does not verify

steps.instruction: Trigger a catalog fetch that returns a signature-invalid payload

observations.expected: Signature verification of the fetched payload fails
The invalid fetched payload is discarded (not written over the trusted cache)
The previously cached, last-verified catalog is served instead

cases.title: Reliability: seeded and installed plugins load 100% offline and new installs fail gracefully
preconditions: App has completed first run online
A set of seeded plugins and previously-installed plugins is present
Network to the marketplace is unreachable for the duration of the run

steps.instruction: Cold-start the app offline repeatedly across the verification run and load the Plugins view each time

observations.expected: Seeded plugins load successfully on 100% of offline starts
Previously-installed plugins load successfully on 100% of offline starts
The app never degrades to zero plugins on any offline start

steps.instruction: Attempt a new (non-seeded) plugin install on each offline start

observations.expected: Each new-install attempt surfaces a clear "marketplace unreachable" error
No new-install attempt crashes the app or leaves it in a broken state

steps.instruction: Confirm verification posture while serving the cached/seed catalog

observations.expected: Verification stays fail-closed throughout (no unverified plugin is treated as verified)

cases.title: End-to-end offline journey: go offline, seeded plugins still work, new install fails clearly
preconditions: App has completed first run online and cached a last-verified signed catalog under ~/.roubo/marketplace/
At least one seeded plugin is running and Jira is a non-seeded, not-yet-installed plugin

steps.instruction: Disconnect the network so the hosted marketplace is unreachable
Launch the app and open the Plugins view

observations.expected: The app loads without crashing and the plugin list is non-empty

steps.instruction: Read the offline warning banner

observations.expected: Banner reads that the marketplace is unreachable and the last verified catalog (fetched 2h ago) is shown, with new installs paused until back online

steps.instruction: Locate the seeded plugin and confirm it is operational offline

observations.expected: The seeded plugin shows Running and can be used without network

steps.instruction: Click Install on the Jira plugin

observations.expected: The install is blocked with a clear error: "Can't install while the marketplace is unreachable"
The app does not crash and the seeded plugin remains Running

steps.instruction: Reconnect the network and re-open the Plugins view

observations.expected: The offline banner clears and the live catalog is fetched, verified, and cached
Installing Jira now succeeds (new installs are no longer paused)

cases.title: Packaged app contains no plugin source dirs, only resources/seed built artifacts
preconditions: The app has been packaged with the marketplace-based (de-bundled) build pipeline
app-packaging downloaded the pinned seed artifacts into resources/seed/ at package time

steps.instruction: Inspect the packaged app's resources/ directory tree (inside and outside the asar)

observations.expected: No resources/plugins/ source directory is present anywhere in the package
No plugin src/ trees are shipped (no github-com/src, process/src, or database/src)

steps.instruction: Inspect the resources/seed/ directory of the packaged app

observations.expected: resources/seed/ contains the signed seed catalog.json plus exactly the three built tarballs github-com, process, and database (<id>-<version>.tgz)
Each seed tarball contains a built dist/index.js plus roubo-plugin.yaml/package.json/README, with no src/ and no node_modules

steps.instruction: Search the packaging code path for the legacy BUNDLED_PLUGIN_IDS source-copy used by copy-resources

observations.expected: The BUNDLED_PLUGIN_IDS source-copy path that copied plugins/<id> into resources/plugins/ is removed/replaced by the seed-artifact download path

cases.title: Functional proof: app ships no executable plugin source, seed install provides the three defaults
preconditions: A fresh install of the de-bundled app on a machine with no prior ~/.roubo/plugins seed marker

steps.instruction: Confirm the app binary/resources carry no plugins/ source dir before first launch

observations.expected: There is no in-app plugins/ source to discover; the only plugin payload present is the resources/seed/ built artifacts

steps.instruction: Launch the app for the first time and let plugin-manager.seedFromBundled() run

observations.expected: The three seed artifacts are installed into ~/.roubo/plugins as built plugins (each with a runnable dist/index.js)
A seed-completed marker is written so seeding is idempotent on subsequent launches

steps.instruction: List installed plugins after seeding completes

observations.expected: github-com, process, and database are present and discoverable; ghe and jira-self-hosted are absent (marketplace-only, not seeded)

cases.title: Upgrade drops prior bundled installs and the seed provides the three defaults fresh
preconditions: A user is on a prior bundled build with bundled-discovered installs of github-com, process, and database
The user upgrades to the marketplace-based de-bundled build

steps.instruction: Record the prior bundled-discovered plugin set and the seed marker state before upgrade
Perform the upgrade and launch the de-bundled build for the first time

observations.expected: The clean-break upgrade step drops the prior bundled-discovered installs (they are not carried over in place)

steps.instruction: Let the first-run seed step run on the upgraded build

observations.expected: seedFromBundled() installs github-com, process, and database fresh from resources/seed into ~/.roubo/plugins as built artifacts
The three seeded plugins start and run after the upgrade

steps.instruction: Confirm the seed marker is written and re-launch the app

observations.expected: Seeding does not run again on the second launch (idempotent via the marker)

cases.title: A non-seeded plugin removed by the clean break is reinstallable from the marketplace
preconditions: A user had a non-seeded plugin (e.g. ghe) bundled-discovered on the prior build
The user has upgraded to the de-bundled build and first-run seeding has completed
The hosted marketplace catalog is reachable and verifies

steps.instruction: Confirm ghe is absent after upgrade (dropped by the clean break, not seeded)

observations.expected: ghe is not present in ~/.roubo/plugins after the upgrade and seed step

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace UI and locate ghe in the verified catalog

observations.expected: ghe is listed in the marketplace as installable

steps.instruction: Install ghe from the marketplace

observations.expected: ghe downloads, verifies, and installs as a built artifact and runs (no missing-entry)

cases.title: Regression/parity: seeded github-com behaves identically to the previously-bundled build
preconditions: The seeded github-com artifact from resources/seed is installed on the de-bundled build
A reference baseline of the previously-bundled github-com behavior is captured

steps.instruction: Spawn the seeded github-com plugin and read its manifest and exposed capabilities

observations.expected: The seeded github-com manifest, plugin id, kind (integration), and declared capabilities match the previously-bundled build exactly

steps.instruction: Run the parity test exercising github-com's integration actions (issue listing/assign and status-category discovery) against both the seeded and prior-bundled builds

observations.expected: Every action's request/response shape and result is identical between seeded and previously-bundled github-com
0 regressions are reported across the seeded vs bundled comparison

cases.title: Config-load regression: existing roubo.yaml with process/database bindings loads and starts identically
preconditions: An existing roubo.yaml with component bindings to the process and database plugins exists unchanged
The de-bundled build is installed and the three seed plugins are present

steps.instruction: Load the existing unchanged roubo.yaml on the de-bundled build

observations.expected: The config parses with no errors and no migration is attempted on the file
The process and database component bindings resolve to the seeded plugins exactly as before (the Role-toggle removal does not alter plugin bindings)

steps.instruction: Start a bench whose components are bound by the existing config

observations.expected: The process and database components start identically to the prior bundled build (same lifecycle, same effective component configuration)

steps.instruction: Compare the started components' effective configuration against the prior-bundled baseline

observations.expected: Existing component configs start identically; 0 config-load regressions

cases.title: No config/instance migration occurs by design on upgrade
preconditions: A prior bundled build had per-plugin instance state and config carried with the in-place bundled installs
The user upgrades to the de-bundled build

steps.instruction: Capture the prior build's per-plugin instance/config state, then upgrade and let the clean-break + seed steps run

observations.expected: No migration step reads, copies, or transforms the prior bundled installs' plugin instance state or config into the new installs

steps.instruction: Inspect the freshly seeded github-com/process/database installs after upgrade

observations.expected: The seeded plugins are fresh installs (clean break), not migrated copies; no inherited instance state is present
The upgrade completes without error despite no migration path existing (no crash, no partial-migration state)

cases.title: Edge: a user who had ghe bundled before upgrade finds it gone and reinstallable from the marketplace
preconditions: A user was on a prior bundled build with ghe bundled-discovered and configured
The user upgrades to the de-bundled build

steps.instruction: Upgrade and complete first-run seeding, then look for ghe

observations.expected: ghe is gone after upgrade because it is not part of the three-plugin seed set (only github-com/process/database are seeded)

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace and reinstall ghe from the hosted catalog

observations.expected: ghe is listed and installs as a verified built artifact, then runs

steps.instruction: Re-add ghe's configuration manually (no migration carried it over) and use it

observations.expected: After manual reconfiguration ghe behaves normally; the clean break required a fresh setup, which is the expected by-design behavior

cases.title: Edge: re-running the upgrade/seed twice is idempotent and does not re-drop or re-seed
preconditions: The de-bundled build has already completed its clean-break upgrade and first-run seed (marker present)

steps.instruction: Relaunch the app and let the seed/clean-break logic evaluate again

observations.expected: Seeding is skipped because the seed-completed marker is present (no re-seed)
The clean-break drop does not re-run against already-marketplace-managed installs (no marketplace-installed plugins are dropped)

steps.instruction: Confirm a user-initiated marketplace install made after seeding survives the relaunch

observations.expected: A plugin the user installed from the marketplace after seeding is retained, not mistaken for a prior bundled install and dropped

cases.title: e2e upgrade journey: bundled dropped, seed provides three defaults, a removed plugin reinstalled from marketplace
preconditions: A user is on the prior bundled build with github-com, process, database, and ghe all bundled-discovered and in use
An existing roubo.yaml binds process and database components
The hosted marketplace catalog is reachable and verifies

steps.instruction: Record the prior bundled plugin set (github-com, process, database, ghe) and the existing roubo.yaml component bindings
Install and launch the marketplace-based de-bundled build for the first time

observations.expected: The clean-break upgrade drops the prior bundled-discovered installs (none are carried over in place)

steps.instruction: Allow first-run seeding to run with no network on the critical path

observations.expected: github-com, process, and database are seeded fresh from resources/seed and the seed-completed marker is written
ghe is NOT seeded (it is marketplace-only)

steps.instruction: Load the existing unchanged roubo.yaml and start a bench whose components bind to process and database

observations.expected: The config loads with no migration and the process/database components start identically to the prior bundled build (NFR-005)

steps.instruction: Open the marketplace and reinstall ghe (the removed non-seeded plugin)

observations.expected: ghe is listed, installs as a verified built artifact, and runs (no missing-entry)

steps.instruction: Verify the seeded github-com behaves identically to its previously-bundled self

observations.expected: github-com's manifest and integration actions match the prior bundled build with 0 regressions

cases.title: Plugin in the separate repo builds resolving @roubo/plugin-sdk from the registry
preconditions: @roubo/plugin-sdk is published to the registry at a pinned version
A plugin project lives in the separate plugins repo (outside the Roubo monorepo)
The plugin's package.json depends on @roubo/plugin-sdk by pinned version, not by a file: workspace ref

steps.instruction: Run a clean install of dependencies in the separate plugin repo

observations.expected: @roubo/plugin-sdk is fetched from the registry at the pinned version
Install completes with no unresolved file:/workspace references

steps.instruction: Grep the plugin's package.json and lockfile for file: refs to @roubo packages

observations.expected: No file: or workspace: dependency on @roubo/plugin-sdk is present

steps.instruction: Run the plugin build (tsup) in the separate repo

observations.expected: Build succeeds with exit code 0
dist/index.js is produced

cases.title: Integration plugin resolves @roubo/shared and @roubo/shared-github and bundles a self-contained dist
preconditions: @roubo/shared and @roubo/shared-github are published to the registry at pinned versions
An integration plugin in the separate repo depends on @roubo/plugin-sdk, @roubo/shared, and @roubo/shared-github by pinned version

steps.instruction: Run a clean install in the integration plugin project

observations.expected: @roubo/shared and @roubo/shared-github resolve from the registry at their pinned versions

steps.instruction: Run the tsup build for the integration plugin

observations.expected: Build succeeds and emits dist/index.js
tsup bundles @roubo/shared and @roubo/shared-github into dist/index.js

steps.instruction: Inspect the built plugin's published package.json dependencies field

observations.expected: dist/index.js declares no runtime dependencies (the bundle is self-contained)

steps.instruction: Load dist/index.js in a host with no @roubo packages installed at runtime

observations.expected: The plugin loads and its integration entrypoint runs without a missing-module error

cases.title: Dependency audit finds no new runtime crypto or supply-chain dependency in the host
preconditions: The Electron host already verifies plugin artifacts with the existing zero-dependency node:crypto ed25519/sha256 verifier
The repo-split change has been applied to the host

steps.instruction: Run a dependency audit of the host's runtime dependency tree after the repo-split change

observations.expected: No oras, cosign, Sigstore, TUF, or OCI client dependency appears in the host runtime tree
No new crypto or supply-chain library is added beyond node:crypto

steps.instruction: Grep the host verifier implementation for its crypto imports

observations.expected: Verification still uses only node:crypto ed25519 and sha256

steps.instruction: Diff the host package.json runtime dependencies before vs after the change

observations.expected: No heavyweight signing or supply-chain runtime dependency was added to the host

cases.title: Building against a stale or missing SDK version fails the plugin build clearly
preconditions: The separate plugin repo pins @roubo/plugin-sdk to a version that does not exist (or has been unpublished) in the registry

steps.instruction: Run a clean install in the plugin repo with the bad pinned SDK version

observations.expected: Install fails with a clear error naming @roubo/plugin-sdk and the unresolvable version
The error does not silently fall back to a cached or different SDK version

steps.instruction: Attempt the plugin build without a successful install

observations.expected: Build aborts with a non-zero exit code and a message indicating @roubo/plugin-sdk is missing

cases.title: Building the same plugin twice in CI yields the same sha256 digest
preconditions: CI normalizes the tarball: sorted entries, fixed mtime, stripped pack metadata, pinned toolchain
A fixed plugin source revision and pinned SDK version are used for both builds

steps.instruction: Run the CI build-and-pack pipeline for the plugin from a clean checkout (build A)
Record the sha256 digest of the packed artifact from build A

observations.expected: A sha256 digest is produced for the build A artifact

steps.instruction: Run the same CI build-and-pack pipeline again from a fresh clean checkout (build B)
Compare the sha256 digest of build B against build A

observations.expected: The build B digest is byte-for-byte equal to the build A digest
Tarball entry order, mtimes, and pack metadata are identical across the two builds

cases.title: Publish-gate self-check rejects a digest that does not match the catalog entry
preconditions: The marketplace catalog records the expected sha256 digest for the published asset
A publish-gate self-check runs after upload

steps.instruction: Publish an asset whose sha256 digest matches its catalog entry

observations.expected: The publish-gate self-check passes and the publish is allowed to complete

steps.instruction: Re-run the publish-gate against an uploaded asset whose bytes were altered so its digest differs from the catalog entry

observations.expected: The self-check fails and blocks the publish
The failure message reports the expected catalog digest versus the actual uploaded digest

cases.title: SDK major bump incompatible with HOST_API_VERSION is rejected at load
preconditions: A plugin is built in the separate repo against a new major version of @roubo/plugin-sdk
The host exposes a HOST_API_VERSION the SDK declares compatibility against

steps.instruction: Build a plugin against an @roubo/plugin-sdk major version newer than the host's HOST_API_VERSION supports

observations.expected: The plugin builds successfully in the separate repo (build is decoupled from host compat)

steps.instruction: Load the resulting plugin into a host whose HOST_API_VERSION predates the SDK major

observations.expected: The host refuses to load the plugin with a clear API-version incompatibility error
The error names the required SDK/host API version and the host's actual version

steps.instruction: Load a plugin built against an SDK major within the host's supported HOST_API_VERSION range

observations.expected: The compatible plugin loads and runs normally

cases.title: Pinned SDK version mismatch between two plugins in the repo does not cross-contaminate builds
preconditions: The separate plugins repo contains two plugins pinning different @roubo/plugin-sdk versions
Both pinned SDK versions exist in the registry

steps.instruction: Install dependencies for both plugins in the separate repo

observations.expected: Each plugin resolves its own pinned @roubo/plugin-sdk version

steps.instruction: Build each plugin in turn and inspect the SDK symbols bundled into each dist/index.js

observations.expected: Each plugin's bundle contains the SDK version it pinned, not the other plugin's version

cases.title: Author journey: build in separate repo against published SDK, CI builds and signs and publishes reproducibly, catalog digest matches
preconditions: @roubo/plugin-sdk, @roubo/shared, and @roubo/shared-github are published to the registry at pinned versions
A plugin author has a plugin project in the separate plugins repo with pinned @roubo dependencies (no file: refs)
CI is configured to build, pack with tarball normalization, sign with node:crypto ed25519, and publish to the marketplace catalog
The host verifier uses node:crypto ed25519/sha256 only

steps.instruction: As the author, run a clean install in the separate plugin repo

observations.expected: All @roubo/* dependencies resolve from the registry at pinned versions with no file: refs

steps.instruction: As the author, run the local tsup build and confirm a self-contained dist/index.js

observations.expected: Build succeeds and dist/index.js bundles the @roubo deps with no runtime dependencies

steps.instruction: Push the plugin source revision to trigger the CI build-and-publish pipeline
Let CI build and pack the plugin with the normalized-tarball steps and pinned toolchain

observations.expected: CI produces a packed artifact and computes its sha256 digest

steps.instruction: Let CI sign the artifact with node:crypto ed25519 and publish it to the marketplace

observations.expected: The artifact is signed and uploaded without adding any oras/cosign/Sigstore/TUF dependency

steps.instruction: Let the publish-gate self-check compare the uploaded asset digest to the catalog entry

observations.expected: The uploaded asset's sha256 digest equals the catalog entry digest and the publish completes

steps.instruction: Re-run CI on the same source revision and re-verify the published artifact digest in the host

observations.expected: The re-built artifact has the same sha256 digest (reproducible build)
The host re-verifies the catalog digest and signature using node:crypto ed25519/sha256 only

cases.title: Components editor shows no legacy Role (Process/Database) toggle
preconditions: A project with at least one configured component is registered
At least one component plugin (Process, Database) is installed

steps.instruction: Open the project's Components editor

observations.expected: The editor renders the list of configured components

steps.instruction: Inspect the controls available for a component

observations.expected: No 'Role' toggle and no Process/Database switch is present anywhere in the editor
No control edits the deprecated component.type field
The component's configuration is presented in terms of its bound plugin only

cases.title: Errored component plugin surfaces its real lastError in the banner
preconditions: A project has a component bound to a component plugin
That plugin's record has lastError { code: 'missing-entry', message: 'Plugin entry file not found: ./dist/index.js ...' }

steps.instruction: Open the Components editor for the project with the errored plugin

observations.expected: A red errored-plugin banner is shown for the affected component

steps.instruction: Read the banner text

observations.expected: The banner shows the plugin's real lastError code (missing-entry) and message (Plugin entry file not found: ./dist/index.js ...)
The banner does NOT show the hardcoded 'Plugin failed to start after 3 restart attempts' copy
The banner does NOT show the 'Showing your last successful issue snapshot' line

cases.title: Add a component by binding an installed component plugin and its schema fields render
preconditions: A project is open in the Components editor
The Process and Database component plugins are installed

steps.instruction: Open the Add-component panel

observations.expected: An Add-component panel opens with a 'Component plugin' selector

steps.instruction: Open the 'Component plugin' selector

observations.expected: The selector lists only installed component plugins (Process, Database)
No standalone Role/type option is offered separate from a plugin binding

steps.instruction: Select the Database component plugin

observations.expected: The bound plugin's config-schema fields render in the panel
The component's plugin binding is set to plugin: { id: <database-plugin-id> }

cases.title: Switching the bound component plugin re-renders schema-driven fields
preconditions: The Add-component panel is open
The Process and Database component plugins are installed

steps.instruction: Select the Process component plugin in the 'Component plugin' selector

observations.expected: The Process plugin's config-schema fields render

steps.instruction: Change the selection to the Database component plugin

observations.expected: The Process plugin fields are replaced by the Database plugin's config-schema fields
The plugin binding updates to the Database plugin id

cases.title: Errored integration plugin banner keeps the issue-snapshot line; component plugin omits it
preconditions: An integration plugin is in an errored state with a real lastError
A component plugin is in an errored state with a real lastError

steps.instruction: View the errored banner for the integration plugin

observations.expected: The integration plugin banner shows its real lastError code and message
The integration plugin banner may include the issue-snapshot line, since it applies to integration plugins

steps.instruction: View the errored banner for the component plugin

observations.expected: The component plugin banner shows its real lastError code and message
The component plugin banner omits the issue-snapshot line entirely

cases.title: Add-component selector lists only installed component plugins (cannot configure an uninstalled type)
preconditions: Only the Process component plugin is installed
The Database component plugin is NOT installed

steps.instruction: Open the Add-component panel and open the 'Component plugin' selector

observations.expected: The selector lists Process only and does not list Database

steps.instruction: Attempt to configure a Database component

observations.expected: There is no way to add a Database component with no plugin installed
The user cannot create a component decoupled from a plugin binding (no 'database with no plugin' state)

cases.title: Component plugin banner never renders the issue-snapshot line
preconditions: A component plugin is in an errored state with a real lastError

steps.instruction: Open the errored banner for the component plugin and scan its full text

observations.expected: The banner does not contain the phrase 'issue snapshot' or any last-successful-snapshot wording
The banner shows only the plugin's real lastError plus the recovery actions (Reinstall / View logs)

cases.title: Banner never shows '3 restart attempts' when the real error is missing-entry
preconditions: A component plugin's record has lastError { code: 'missing-entry', message: 'Plugin entry file not found: ./dist/index.js ... reinstall it from the marketplace' }

steps.instruction: Open the errored banner and read its full text

observations.expected: The banner does not contain the hardcoded 'Plugin failed to start after 3 restart attempts' string
The banner reflects the missing-entry message including the './dist/index.js' path and 'reinstall it from the marketplace' guidance
Reinstall and View logs actions are offered

cases.title: Banner with a long, multi-line lastError renders without breaking layout
preconditions: A component plugin's record has a very long, multi-line lastError message

steps.instruction: Open the errored banner for the plugin with the long multi-line lastError

observations.expected: The full lastError code and message are presented (wrapped or scrollable), not silently truncated to nothing
The banner layout does not overflow its container or push the Reinstall / View logs actions off-screen
Surrounding editor controls remain usable and correctly positioned

cases.title: Component with an empty/absent lastError shows no errored banner
preconditions: A component is bound to a healthy component plugin with no lastError set

steps.instruction: Open the Components editor for the healthy component

observations.expected: No errored-plugin banner is rendered for the healthy component
No leftover hardcoded '3 restart attempts' or 'issue snapshot' copy appears anywhere

cases.title: End-to-end: open project settings, add a component, bind an installed plugin, configure, and save
preconditions: A registered project is available
The Process and Database component plugins are installed

steps.instruction: Open the project's settings and navigate to the Components editor

observations.expected: The Components editor opens with no legacy Role (Process/Database) toggle visible

steps.instruction: Click Add component to open the Add-component panel

observations.expected: The Add-component panel opens with a 'Component plugin' selector

steps.instruction: Open the 'Component plugin' selector

observations.expected: The selector lists only installed component plugins (Process, Database)

steps.instruction: Select the Database component plugin

observations.expected: The bound plugin's config-schema fields render in the panel

steps.instruction: Fill in the required schema-driven configuration fields with valid values
Click Save

observations.expected: The component is persisted with plugin: { id: <database-plugin-id> } as its binding
The deprecated component.type field is not written for the new component
The new component appears in the Components editor list bound to the Database plugin

cases.title: End-to-end: an errored component plugin guides recovery via real lastError and Reinstall
preconditions: A project has a component bound to a component plugin
That plugin's record has lastError { code: 'missing-entry', message: 'Plugin entry file not found: ./dist/index.js ... reinstall it from the marketplace' }

steps.instruction: Open the project's settings and navigate to the Components editor

observations.expected: A red errored-plugin banner is shown for the affected component

steps.instruction: Read the banner content

observations.expected: The banner shows the real lastError (missing-entry, ./dist/index.js, 'reinstall it from the marketplace')
The banner omits the issue-snapshot line and the '3 restart attempts' hardcoded copy

steps.instruction: Confirm the recovery affordances are present

observations.expected: Reinstall and View logs actions are shown

steps.instruction: Click Reinstall

observations.expected: The reinstall flow for the affected plugin is initiated
